issues.main_description_html:
Sign In Page

Source: auth-pages-brainstorming.md
Area: Sign in
Audience: UI/UX Designer, UI UX Pro Max

Goal

Make sign in feel like returning to an indoor mapping workspace while keeping the flow fast for existing users.

Updates

  • Update the headline to connect sign-in with the user's indoor mapping workspace.
  • Add supporting copy about managing 3D floor maps, digital twins, and wayfinding projects.
  • Improve the error message for invalid credentials.
  • Improve the email-not-verified path so users know exactly what to do next.
  • Add a product visual showing saved building maps, active routes, and levels.
  • Keep forgot-password and create-account links easy to find.

Design Notes

  • Returning users should not feel slowed down by marketing content.
  • The visual should suggest saved projects, active routes, and multi-level maps.
  • Error messages should be calm, specific, and recoverable.

RESULT

Sign Up Page

Source: auth-pages-brainstorming.md
Area: Sign up
Audience: UI/UX Designer, UI UX Pro Max

Goal

Make account creation feel like starting an indoor mapping workspace and clearly communicate the product value before the user reaches the app.

Updates

  • Update the headline to position account creation as starting an indoor mapping workspace.
  • Add product-focused copy about turning 2D floor plans into 3D maps and wayfinding experiences.
  • Add a short trust/reassurance line near the submit button.
  • Show password guidance before the user submits.
  • Improve the post-sign-up transition into email verification.
  • Use the strongest visual story here: 2D floor plan transforming into a 3D multi-level map.

Design Notes

  • This page should carry the strongest product storytelling.
  • The form must remain short and approachable.
  • The visual should make floor plan conversion understandable at a glance.

RESULT

Forgot Password Page

Source: auth-pages-brainstorming.md
Area: Forgot password
Audience: UI/UX Designer, UI UX Pro Max

Goal

Make password recovery feel calm, secure, and easy to complete.

Updates

  • Make the page feel calm and recovery-focused, not like an error page.
  • Add clearer copy about sending a secure reset link.
  • Keep the privacy-safe success message: if an account exists, a link was sent.
  • Add reassurance that reset links expire for security.
  • Add a product-related recovery visual, such as a route reconnecting.
  • Keep the back-to-sign-in action visible.

Design Notes

  • Avoid alarming language.
  • The success state should protect user privacy.
  • The visual should imply restored access without becoming a generic security illustration.

RESULT

issues.display_title: Issue #168: Update Dashboard Layout UI Design
issues.main_description_html:
Docs

dashboard-layout-brainstorming.md

Goal

Update the dashboard layout so it feels easier, faster, and more visual. The dashboard should immediately display created projects in a grid list and provide clear project tools, list tools, and profile navigation.

Design Success Criteria

  • Users immediately see their created projects after opening the dashboard.
  • Users can identify projects by name, location, status, and thumbnail.
  • Users can quickly search, sort, and filter projects.
  • Users can preview, publish, edit, and delete a project from its card.
  • Navbar clearly communicates MapThru branding and provides profile actions.
  • Empty, loading, error, and no-result states feel polished and helpful.
  • The layout works well on desktop, tablet, and mobile.

RESULT

Verify Email Page

Source: auth-pages-brainstorming.md
Area: Verify email
Audience: UI/UX Designer, UI UX Pro Max

Goal

Make email verification feel like account activation and guide users smoothly into the workspace.

Updates

  • Improve the loading state with clearer copy: verifying email.
  • Improve the success state with a visible confirmation before redirect.
  • Improve the expired or invalid link state with a direct request-new-link action.
  • Add a visual that shows account activation, such as a destination marker lighting up.
  • Make the redirect to the workspace feel intentional and reassuring.

Design Notes

  • The user should understand what is happening during verification.
  • Success should feel like progress into the product.
  • Expired links should have a clear recovery path.

RESULT

Verification Pending Page

Source: auth-pages-brainstorming.md
Area: Verification pending
Audience: UI/UX Designer, UI UX Pro Max

Goal

Make the pending verification page clear, reassuring, and action-oriented while the user waits to activate their workspace.

Updates

  • Update the headline to explain that the user must check their inbox to activate the workspace.
  • Show the masked email address clearly.
  • Add a short explanation of what happens after verification.
  • Add a reminder to check spam or promotions folders.
  • Improve resend email states: idle, sending, sent, rate-limited, and error.
  • Make the cooldown timer easy to understand.
  • Keep the wrong-email/sign-out action available but secondary.
  • Use a visual step path: account created, email sent, email verified, workspace active.

Design Notes

  • The page should reduce confusion after sign-up.
  • The resend action should show clear state feedback.
  • The wrong-email path should be available without competing with the main action.

RESULT

Reset Password Page

Source: auth-pages-brainstorming.md
Area: Reset password
Audience: UI/UX Designer, UI UX Pro Max

Goal

Help users set a new password with clear guidance, strong recovery states, and a smooth return to sign in.

Updates

  • Add clearer states for checking the reset link, invalid link, password form, and success.
  • Add password guidance before validation errors appear.
  • Improve password mismatch and short-password messages.
  • Make expired-link recovery obvious with a request-new-link action.
  • Add a success state that guides the user back to sign in.
  • Use a visual that suggests access restored, such as an activated route or unlocked destination.

Design Notes

  • Every state needs a clear next action.
  • Password rules should be visible before submission.
  • The invalid-link state should not be a dead end.

RESULT
